extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds for all reachable executions.

Key observations:
1. sent is set to true ONLY by t_send_allowed, whose guard is "not opted_out". So at any moment sent becomes true, opted_out is false. It is impossible to set sent=true while opted_out is true (t_send_allowed is disabled in that case).
2. opted_out is monotonic: only t_optout sets it (to true), and no transition ever resets it to false. Once true it stays true.
3. t_optout sets opted_out:=true AND simultaneously sent:=false, so the instant opted_out becomes true, sent is false — the invariant is established at the transition point.
4. Once opted_out is true, the only events that can fire are: optout (sets sent:=false), send_attempt -> t_send_blocked (sets sent:=false; t_send_allowed is guarded off), and tick (sets sent:=false). Every one of these keeps sent=false. No reachable transition can make sent true while opted_out is true.

Therefore in every reachable state, opted_out=true implies sent=false. No counterexample exists within (or beyond) the 12-event bound. The property holds.
